channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 238 replies — 311.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 238 • Updated: 2026-05-24 11:59 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264605
>>62260997
based

62264672
>>62264595
You’re supposed to slave away like everyone else.
lol.
“Society” is like 95% worker bees with no choice but to toll.

62264833
>>62259935
I dislike people cooking for me. I don't know how I will be able to voice this to my future wife

62264838
>Aw geez guys I have 76 years of spending saved up at 35 years old do I have enough to retire?
Uhh, yes. Congrats. Although he'd be better off putting most of it in a trust with a reasonable $40k/yr drawdown and pursue marriage as a trustfund kid, and not have to worry about divorce after that.

62264839
>>62260039
>>62262037
These comics were highly respected in the olden days. Remember this one?

62264927
>>62259968
Marry a rich bride, retard. Fucking low class roastoid marrying nigger, just kill yourself loser.

62264930
>>62259980
Where do all you currycels come from? Unbelievable.

62265011
>>62260025
Surrogacy. Be a single dad.

62265250
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s

62265283
>>62259968
>marry
>because one divorce
Then don't marry?
>muh keedz
You can have them without marrying
>muh common law
Move somewhere where that's not a thing, or recycle through women.
>>62259993
>monk
You can enjoy life without living paycheck to paycheck. Sex is basically free if you find the right girl. Packing homemade food and eating somewhere new every weekend costs less than fast food.

62265591
>>62260376
>who's going to pay for my kids now
He will through taxes

62265814
>goy wants to be free
uh oh, stinky

62265828
>>62260306
You just need to have some imagination to make it feel more fun but most men are retarded simp faggots and they need to rely on a woman's bullshit to feel good when they fuck. Women learn to turn it into a fun artificial challenge so it hypes you up.

62265831
>>62263540
>you know, actual
Normalfag subhuman brain rot. You're too retarded to enjoy your free time. You have nothing to teach to your spawn.

62265857
>>62263428
Normalfags can only think in binary terms. They're literal cattle and they turn suicidal without a master's guidance. When normalfags get rich they go insane and start coping with drugs and prostitutes until they die of STDs, OD or suicide. They can't handle the freedom.

62265866
>>62264003
Ok it's not the same, it's a matter of time.
If you've been working for a year and don't have 10k saved you're fucked. If you've been working for 6 years and don't have $100k you're also fucked. And yes $10k is more like a short-term emergency cushion against day-to-day issues where $100k is more like a longer term yearish cushion that may be enough to comfortably make small investments in one's business or other opportunities without threatening that cushion.

Basically the normie never saves that much. Rather than thinking "I need to get enough money together so my investments pay for everything I want forever without drawing down the balance" normies just think about making payments for next month.

62265872
>>62262791
The egregore of society doesn't even give a shit that you think it doesn't deserve it. It just imports desperate people from war torn poverty stricken shitholes instead.

62265874
>>62262844
>>62261211
How is offering the system your children to use as taxcattle a fuck you to it?

62265878
>>62260844
>debt free
Meme. I'm debtmaxxing harder than the US of Pissrael.

62265882
>>62261114
(You)r penis is too pure to allow it to be defiled by a wench's vaginal secretions.

62265887
>>62261238
>swingers
military fag brainrot leads to cockrot
>>62262206
they're trying to buck break us into flipping burgers and it's working

62265888
>>62259935
You know you can easily have biological children without marriage right?

Just fucking cum in some young woman and keep doing it until ahe misses a period. If that makes you fags squeamish just pay some surrogate to carry your child while you enjoy escorts or casual dating and sex.

It really isn't that hard, and after the surrogate pushes your child out it'll be the last time you need to hear a woman yell at you ever again lol.

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

item.title: why does this keep happening — 66 replies — 264.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-05-24 12:44 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62268417
>>62263230
Why should it? The token holds no function other than being dumped on retail. Check CCIP tracker fees. None of those integrations actually use CCIP.
>inb4 those are private
Riddle me this then: why? All these institutions/ big players publicly claim to use it, why obfuscate the actual usage? There isn't any.

62268439
>>62268417
>Why should it?
Because it would've if Bitcoin didn't stop it.

>The token holds no function other than being dumped on retail.
Chainlink nodes need the token to post data. Nothing works without it.

>All these institutions/ big players publicly claim to use it, why obfuscate the actual usage? There isn't any.
What institutions are using typically isn't CCIP, it's mostly price feeds etc.
DTCC for instance is going to go live this year with CRE.

62268471
>>62268439
>Because it would've if Bitcoin didn't stop it.
Schizoid speculation at best.

>>62268439
>Chainlink nodes need the token to post data. Nothing works without it.
Errr, payment abstraction, they can pay in whatever currency they want. Nodes will dump the link tokens the second they receive them for stables, making the buy/sell of link tokens demand neutral.

>it's mostly price feeds etc
The thing link is widely known for, and the price feed market is considered so low revenue that nobody else even bothers to compete. The game-changer was supposed to be cross-chain traffic.

62268476
>>62268471
There were literal dozens of projects trying to compete with Chainlink on price feeds kek

They all died because Chainlink is the standard

62268479
>>62268471
>Schizoid speculation at best.
lol look at the pic in op. It's an empirical fact that Bitcoin stopped Link from going over $10.

>they can pay in whatever currency they want.
I said the nodes need the token to post data.

>the price feed market is considered so low revenue
Weren't you fudders crying about CLL "stealing hundreds of millions"? How can that be when their revenue is so low?

>nobody else even bothers to compete
Dozens tried, including Compound and Coinbase.
And they pretty much all went down in flames.

62268487
>>62268476
Is this lucrative price feed revenue in the room with us now? My point is the feeds are worth less, and link needs CCIP volume to move.

>>62268479
>It's an empirical fact
ironically take your meds. people aren't out to get you.

How can that be when their revenue is so low?
Do i need to explain ill-gotten gains vs legitimate business practices?

62268499
>>62268487
>ironically take your meds. people aren't out to get you.
Whether this was intentional or not doesn't change the fact that Bitcoin dumping the picosecond Link hit $10 stopped Link from going above $10.

>Do i need to explain ill-gotten gains vs legitimate business practices?
Whether ill-gotten or legitimate, hundreds of millions are hundreds of millions.

62268507
>>62268499
>Bitcoin dumping the picosecond
You could make 100's of the these correlations about any other token. Link is only special because it has many eyes and schizo's watching it to make those correlations. It doesn't make it true.

>>62268499
>Whether ill-gotten or legitimate
Anon, how is the company basing its entire identity on truth>trust supposed to act this way and maintain any form of integrity?

62268545
>>62268487
>ironically take your meds. people aren't out to get you.
they literally are, it has been proved for years now you gaslighting niggerkike

62268547
>>62268507
so show one example of it happening to any other coin

62268557
>>62268545
This is not an exclusive thought to just link. Literally every altcoin community that rivals in schizo's think this. You're just in the link echochamber.

>>62268547
Here's an xrp fag thinking the same thing. I'll keep going with other tokens as well.

62268560
eth

62268563
xrp again, different anon

62268565
>>62268545
i can almost gurantee you these anons are repeating the same narratives in their respective echo chambers

62268568
ada

62268569
>>62268557
>>62268560
>>62268563
Where are the screenshots of BTC dumping right after X coin hitting specific price number or having massive news announced?

62268571
>>62268569
I'm not making a chart nor delving their cesspit to find their charts. The point stands that every community thinks there is some conspiracy against them. This is how all schizo's think.

62268578
>>62268569
here, xrp different anon saying it dumps on good news

62268582
>>62268569
again xrp different anon, same phrase

62268594
>>62268571
>>62268578
>>62268582

>11 pbtid

Okay so no screenshots, got it

62268596
sry that was the same anon

62268598
>>62268594
>telling me to post less
>asking for proof
>get provided screenshots
>ok so no screenshots
are you retarded?

62268653
>>62268598
We are all still waiting for your evidence like the OP or.. this video

https://youtu.be/8nEPWUFgfvk

62268689
>>62268507
>You could make 100's of the these correlations about any other token.
You've been saying this for years but you never managed to do it.
Because it's simply not true.

62268691
>>62268557
>>62268560
>>62268563
>>62268568
>>62268578
>>62268582

post comparative charts not tweets

62268729
>>62268653
Honestly, I'm not finding any chart examples through my brief trip on Twitter where they put the news overlay onto the chart of BTC dumping. Clearly, you guys keep better incelsheets. Yet those anons keep saying it, and they believe it, as you all do

>>62263230
This chart isn't proof of anything. Link had a good day, and BTC dumped...happens to many cryptos
>>62268545
I made this one with Solana just now, BTC top, Sol bottom. You can make the same mental parallels that btc dumped to suppress it, but the truth is it just follows the market, which is BTC.

62268734
>awww man every time cro starts to take off they dump btc
Can you hear them saying that just like you do? Also looking for more identical charts to link, and the first one didn't line up that well, sorry.

62268743
>>62265193
This one's good. Atom, exact same spike link did right, same time, same day. Did btc also dump to suppress atom doing the same pattern as link. With this I rest my case.

62268749
>>62268743
so atom hit $2.15 "resistance" and dumped to 42.00

62268753
>>62268749
just how link went to 9.15, it was not a special "ohh the manipulation, i got the screenie" event

item.title: GIGA HAPPENING! — 12 replies — 43.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
GIGA HAPPENING!

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-24 12:28 UTC

OP

Linkies officially roundtripped 9 years of gains against Bitcoin.

Congrats on this, seriously

Early replies (first 3)

62265402
At this point, I'd rather buy Trump memecoin than Link. Seriously

62265403
>>62265398
Seems like a good time to slurp

62265410
>>62265403
>buying a rugged 2017 shitcoin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265415
>>62265398
damn this is absolutely brutal, officially no gains vs. bitcoin.

62265425
>>62265403
This. Dont underestimate the power of positive thinking

62265445
>>62265398
Works on my machine.

62265450
>>62265445
>log chart
>still looks atrocious

62265487
>>62265450
LINK outperformed BTC.

62265501
>>62265487
why didn't you sell in 2020?

62265558
>>62265501
Why didn't you buy SanDisk last year?

62265583
Truly the cucks of crypto

62268739
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

item.title: >Housings are afforda-ACK — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Housings are afforda-ACK

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-24 12:43 UTC

OP

>Housings are afforda-ACK

https://x.com/Mericamemed/status/2058050291853865192

https://xcancel.com/Mericamemed/status/2058050291853865192

Early replies (first 3)

62266420
>>62266289
>Housings
How's the weather in Islamabad, Usman?

62266523
everything on twitter is fake

62268258
>>62266289
Clearly a pajeet account, moron.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62268260
>>62266289
housings huh?

learn to engrish good

62268719
>>62266523
Very real saar now click and buy my product.

62268721
>>62266523
Yeah if it was real his camera would've been stolen.

62268731
>>62266289
A triplex for 60k is wild

62268752
>>62266289
>x.com/Mericamemed
Someone check and post the location of the account by adding /about to this URL

item.title: There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP — 34 replies — 33.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-05-24 11:43 UTC

OP

There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a WS VIP

Early replies (first 3)

62267362
Im not poor I have a pressure washing business

62267365
>>62267358
decided to try again with the name covered this time?

the old thread is still up, we can see the name on the card

even if it gets taken down, it's in the archives

>The surname Kepnes is of Ashkenazi Jewish origin, with roots in Eastern European communities (such as Ukraine and Poland).

62267382
>>62267358
whats that one? need over 100k in account or something? what's it do?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267645
>>62267358
wow, a 2% cashback card. literally everyone offers that, it's basically the minimum nowadays. why do you think this is a flex?

62267652
>>62267382
apparently if you have 100k in your account they give you a metal version of the card and waive the annual fee. given that OP is holding a plastic card we know that he's a broke idiot, and also he bites his nails.

62267666
>>62267645
it's one of the best cards in cuckada just below the amex cobalt before it got nerfed. It is a flex because a VIP requires you to have a 150k minimum income.

62267670
>>62267358
damn nigga you are one intense nail biter

62267708
>>62267652
>Wow $100,000 in a bank account!!! What a big deal!

Lmao tell me you're poor without telling me you're poo

62267730
>>62267708
i just know that's the min to get a fancy card through WS, no one said it was a le huge amount
your comment says more about you then anything

62267834
>>62267708
100k to waive the fee, but you still need minimum 80k income for the visa infinite. For the Visa Infinite Privilege you need 150k income. Also it's a visa infinite privilege so it is a metal card

62267839
>>62267834
metal cards aren't really that special anymore, even i have one.
>t. poor

62267900
>>62267358
>KEKY CANADIAN CREDIT CARDS

LMAOOOOOOOOOOO

62267941
>>62267358
is the interest rate on these goyim ID cards still like 25%? i can think of nothing more cucked and slavish than being proud of something like this.

62267964
>>62267941
do you even understand how credit cards work you dumb retard? its interest free money as long as you pay it off in 30 days

62267992
>>62267964
yeah i'm asking if you're making over 100k, care about dumbass credit cards that grant you elite perks like airport lounge access (lol....lmao even), AND carry a balance (because you will, because you're a dumb faggot who cares about shit like this), are you still paying a 25% interest rate?

62268001
>>62267992
generally no. After the 12 month introductory period the interest rate for a millionaire is in the range of about 6%

this is bumped up a bit by the annual fee.

62268003
>>62268001
thank you. that is an actual perk.

62268004
>>62268001
I'm still shocked there are retards out there who pay an annual fee for credit cards. Unbelievable.

62268005
>>62267992
it also depends on how much you spend

they charge merchants about 3% for a processing fee, so the interest rates to a consumer can be zero or even negative if you spend enough money. They'll make their interest off the vendor. And rich people can buy tens or even hundreds of thousands on credit for free while the card company still makes bank.

technically you're still paying the merchant's share of 3%, but nominally you're paying zero.

62268007
>>62268003
6% is high
I can borrow against my house right now for less than 3% so cards aren't super attractive unless you're writing off the interest

which most rich people do.

62268008
>>62268005
yeah different hustle same game just to drive spend on the card makes sense.

62268010
>>62268004
>I'm still shocked there are retards out there who pay an annual fee for credit cards. Unbelievable.
it's an entirely different calculus when you're
1. writing off the fees on your taxes, probably as a business expense
2. getting perks and upgrades in exchange for the fee

rich people math isn't the same as normal budgeting. taxes become a lot more important the higher you go

62268015
>>62268010
an example

say I'm a millionaire business owner taxed at 50% (35% plus 15% SE tax ignoring the overlap cutoff).

If I pocket $100 I owe $50 to the IRS

If I waste $100 on a product and get $75 worth of perks in exchange, I came out ahead. Because I lost $100 but gained $75 tax-free, which is better than only having $50 after tax

this is the strategy behind lots of business expenses. I'm technically throwing money away, but I get more in return than if I had kept the money and paid taxes on it.

62268020
>>62268015
this is also the basis of most B2B sales

If you pay me $100 for a product and I deliver $75 worth of product I made $25 and you still came out ahead because after taxes you would have only kept $50 anyways.

businesses are fine with spending money on stuff people would consider pointless because it's cheaper than paying taxes

62268022
>>62267358
I have the BMO World Elite Mastercard with 5% cashback.

Questrade will also be launching a couple of credit card products this fall, one of them will be the Questrade Reserve Card for their most elite members, which will have 6% cashback.

62268044
>>62268022
why do the rich just get richer in our system?
what's with the invisible poor tax on everyone ontop of normal taxes?

62268049
>>62268044
rich people find scalable free money hacks while wagies are on a fixed income that can't be increased beyond the number of hours they can work.

the reason the poor pay so much more for everything is simple. That extra poor tax is exactly where the rich people get their money

62268055
>>62267834
I have this card, it’s not a flex. 150k cad is hobo tier in this country, also nobody will every recognize the card so hardly a status symbol even if you consider it one

62268064
>>62268055
is the cutoff in CAD?

I bought a timeshare once that had a cutoff of $70k USD to apply. The cutoff in CAD was higher. Whatever the exchange rate was.

62268072
>>62268064
Yes, Wealthsimple is for Canadians only. We have the most kiked banks so this platform is good by comparison. Still can’t do stuff like mortgages, business loans, big purchases or withdraw for physical cash though so still need a regular bank to off ramp to if you want to use your money for these things.

62268085
>>62268072
interesting. You guys are even more fucked on real estate than we are from what I've seen here

62268623
>>62268022
>5% on groceries capped at $500 spending per month then it goes to 1%
KEK

62268692
Is this a marketing thread? Should I get this? I just think it would be tedious to transfer over money to my ws from my main bank to use it unless I can have it autopay from the other account which I'm sure I can? I'm pulling around 250k so I can get the fancy version too. I feel like I never use any of those perks, maybe the 2% back would be ok? I just have a basic bitch scotia momentum right now.

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 86 replies — 28.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-05-24 09:43 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260090
>>62259745
He’s buying

62261050
>>62257679
BTC infrastructure is basically trying to solve that balance now: preserving self custody while making native BTC more usable across broader financial systems. Maybe you’re just not updated yet.

62261057
>>62252472
Buy btc for many reasons anon.

62261131
>>62257675
*sends 5 million dollars worth of gold across the world in 5 minutes for a nominal fee*

62261260
>>62261050
It's kinda scary to go deeper with that
>>62261131
Plus big chance for theft

62261264
>>62258793
Hello I'm here

62261340
>>62257392
>>62261260
Nothing wrong with being cautious, just don’t treat all BTCFi the same. Big names like a16zcrypto, Ledger, GoMining and others wouldn’t even touch it if everything was complete garbage.

62261342
>>62252488
50k? Nooo way

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

62262278
>>62259397
Buy altcoins too

62262820
>>62261608
No need to rush. People will figure it out eventually. You can already see more attention shifting toward DeFi outside the usual Ethereum-only lane anyway.

62262829
>>62261131
And people still wonder why institutions keep accumulating BTC.

62262834
>>62256924
Depends on their choice tho

62262852
Bitcoin is the future

62262893
>>62262278
>Hbar
>Link
>Rave
>Sei

62264806
>>62262852
And the present

62265221
>>62258793
Devil

62265362
Buy 10btc

62267557
>>62262893
Not bad

62267594
>>62250313
I'd be happy for $500 kek

62268019
I am aiming for having 0,1 BTC by the end of the year (got a nasty 3'000$ tax bill in august and september) but aside from that I'm going all in every month from now.

62268405
>>62260090
with what money

62268430
>>62253499
Checked. I appreciate the assessment.

62268528
>>62268430
When moon?

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 152 replies — 12.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 152 • Updated: 2026-05-24 00:33 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264946
>>62264122
Based

62264980
>>62262862
That's common here in New Zealand as well.
I've seen people turn up with;

A whole cooked chicken and nothing else.
A 12 pack of bread rolls and a tin of Tuna.
One large bag of carrots.
A tin of Baked Beans and no can opener.
A loaf of bread and a smoked chicken breast.
Half a dozen cooked snags.
Plain rice and a chicken drumstick.
Two Sea Urchins.
A cheesy loaf.
A banana bunch.

Last nights dinner is very common.

62265020
>>62262598
you can put a loaf of bread in the fridge to make it last longer, anon

62265030
>>62263602

62265033
>>62263530
they are too retarded to understand that the shiny rocks are what go into their computers to design and make their pokemon cards

62265037
>>62265020
depends on conditions, more important for bread is air tightness and moister.. putting it in some fridges can make it go faster depending.. you can freeze bread and defrost it as you need pretty fast though

62265043
>>62265037
For toast putting the bread in the freezer is just sensible.

62265138
>>62265020
Just stick it in a bread bin like a normal person

62265148
>>62262449
boomer here

I was 30 years old when I could afford to eat at a restaurant for anything other than a special occasion. I was 40 when I could afford to eat at a restaurant whenever I wanted.

younger generations don't get a lot of pity for demanding luxuries we worked our asses off for and they think are just rights every person deserves.

same with houses. And new cars. Earn it. It took us decades of work.

62265153
>>62265138
Look at this fancy fuck with his bread humidor.

62265271
I'm bringing bread and cheese and ham and some fucking canned soup with me to Stockholm next week to beat the Swedish jew and there's nothing you can do to stop me. And some booze in my checked bag. Buying alcohol in Sweden will bankrupt a millionaire.

62265280
>>62262630
To be honest you can make bread on the griddle, so the bread maker is just wasted money.

62265288
>>62265271
Buy Felix' Gulasch when you're there. It is a yummy soup.

62265303
>he doesn’t make his own bread

62265600
>>62265288
We have soup here where I live. I don't need to pay 10€ for a can or jar of Swedish goulash slop.

62265622
>check thread
>It's still an Indian psyop to get you to eat bread for every meal and live like a prisoner and be happy
I hate this board now

62265722
Isn't it obvious this entire thing is just the uber business model being deployed to food?

1. Establish yourself in an area
2. Undercut the competition
3. Make as many people as possible rely on your service
4. Establish a near monopoly
5. Raise prices
6. Profit

I mean people like to point out that the fast food prices went up during covid and blamed the money printing and rising wages. That neglects the people who were serious about the lockdown basically not leaving the house and just ordering delivery over and over until they got used to the idea. Then loan shark services like klarna drops by and offers people a way to extend the use even when you don't have money.

I mean one of the delivery companies literally is Uber.

62266666
>>62262474
Shartistanis pay $5 for a loaf of bread and $12 for 18 bags of crisps? I just bought two loaves for 26p each (maybe 80c total) and isn't glyphosate filled foam rubber

62266673
>>62266666
CHECKED

62266688
>>62262474
he's right, that like $6500 for the year. spent on lunch.

i actually had this conversation with a guy i work with, when he was slinging weed he was going out to eat every night with his girlfriend, he realized he had been spending a fucking fortune on food. i've made every meal i've eaten for the last 4 months.

62266712
>>62262506
it's not going to get better cupcake. we are sprinting head-on into an existential crisis. either AI delivers utopia forever, or it delivers utopia for a year or two then wipes us out. no one knows because no one has time to make sure it's not acting sussy.

62266992
>>62263206
I spend less than 15 dollars a month on my gym membership and the cute mullato at the desk always says hi to me.

62267454
>>62265138
i don't think these do shit, they're not airtight, all they do is stop light from reaching the bread i guess.
i keep mine in the fridge with the bag cinched super tight, lasts for weeks, have no problems.

62267474
>>62262587
This is bullshit
No i will not be explaining

62267477
>>62262773
Is this bmw keys downie from ck?

62267510
>>62262587
bitch it takes 20 minutes to make spaghetti and half of that is sitting down while the water boils.
also, instead of being retarded, why not get a stool or something for your kitchen. shit, get a comfy office chair for all i care.

62267556
>>62264583
>how a retard boomer like Dave becomes worth hundreds of millions of dollars
The same way all of them did. By claiming greater knowledge and understanding and then selling it to you.
I have a six figure net worth and virtually no debt but I can manage my debt and understand the purpose of debt and I understand the economic problems he pretends don't exist and don't crow about people not wanting to literally eat shit for the rest of their lives in hopes that maybe one day they can buy a house that they'll never be able to afford because the same compound interest curve that grows your investments exponentially prices them out of the market exponentially. I'm not high on my own supply and recognize that prices have gone up far faster than wages, so it's not about "You're wasting money going out to eat". Like dude said, even if you're spending $28 for lunch every day, that's still only 10k a year that could be going towards your housing downpayment, but housing downpayments are no longer 20-30k, they're 100-200k.

62267568
>>62263206
What you and a lot of these other faggots don't seem to comprehend is that these subscriptions are part of the social dynamic today.
Yeah, you can stop spending money on netflix or your phone or your gym membership or whatever else you're doing other than eating shit and saving money for two decades for a downpayment, you're missing out on socializing with your community, you're missing out on being able to ask a girl back to netflix and chill, all things that are more important than saving up 50k for a downpayment on trailer that 25 years ago cost $10k. You hear me? A GODDAMNED TRAILER costs six figures today.
These people are not only detached from reality, they're all playing their part in the jewish-libertarian atomization of white society.

62267583
>>62262474
Are Gen Z even aware of that spare tire underneath their trunks?

62267662
>BEST ECONOMY EVER!!! THE DOW IS SO HIGH!!!! HOW DOES GOD EMPEROR TRUMP DO IT!?!??!
>...
>Uh.... guys can you stop whining about the cost of living...? Can't you just eat sawdust mixed with corn syrup or something? Your ruining the MAGA vibes, y'know?

item.title: NTDOY — 66 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-05-24 05:29 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

62261314
Sorry about your hopes of pumping your bags OP, but I'll just leave this here...
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=luiKV1nHB1w

62261355
>>62259371
The switch 2 is overpriced. People who have money don't even know what it is. It also demotes the value of the switch one, which partially went subscription based model. So it was bad business design.

62261416
Tell me the last time they ever release a ground breaking software or hardware? The new pokemon is not a cashgrab.

62261518
anyway, you know when a retard here shill some stock:
1/ he is clueless no amount of shiling here will pump it not even 0.01%

2/therefore it's a bad pick

it's cringe.last retard shilled mc donald like it was some secret gem. The real blackpill is whatever gets shilled here don't buy it or even better sell it if you have some (pokemon cards, random crypto that miraculously pumped x10, nintengoy stocks, mc goynald stock, whatever)

62261624
>>62260035
>It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.
What psychogenic kush are you smoking? It makes a ton of sense. By your logic, a goldsmith can't have created a masterpiece work that he has sold 20 years ago but which is now recognized to be more valuably than his entire shop.

62261625
Value Trap 2: Electric Boogaloo

62261640
>>62259371
I agree. They are the one last big truly JP dev remaining, huge franchise value that's only getting better, the only one console with exclusives, ninety-something % of the serious handheld business with proper customer attachment, JP approach to quality as opposed to deadlines, no DEI, success with theme park now expanding, huge success with the films now.
No reason not to own their stock, I think it's a good 50% undervalued.

62261642
>2009
>Nintendo has suffered a drawdown of 50%
>ZOMG invest in Nintendo!!
>keeps dropping
>10 years to reach the position you entered

Yeah nah, I know a correction when I see it.

62261677
>>62261625
kek.
What is your average OP?
Buying this above 2 is like giving to a charity

62262033
>>62261314
Already priced in

62262739
6months performance : - 45%
1 month perfomance : -12%
today's perfomance : - 0.47%

wow troontendgoy really IS daw new millionaire maker!

62263011
>>62259371
Kids don't buy games anymore. If they play games, it's free shit like fortnite and roblox

62263036
>>62263011
Also they know how to pirate.

62263054
>>62261640
>DEI
Holy shit lmaoo, it's so cringe in vidya DEI is still a hot topic while all other sectors of the economy have moved on 2 years ago. Drenched with pozz.

62263652
>>62262739
>anon argues against buying dips
I reckon you're one of those faggots who buys high and sells low

62263715
>>62259371
People aren't interested in bing bing wahoo anymore.

62263737
Nintendo even fucked over collectors with the Switch 2, I just can't justify buying physical games when they're not even on cart, this shit will have zero resale value in 10 years when the servers are no longer around. And buying digital is retarded unless they're extremely discounted.

62264904
>>62259407
>console era is coming to an end
xbox is dead and playstation is reversing its multiplat strategy so no

62264915
>>62259371
It’s not, Nintendo is failing hard lately. It is a slop company of shit now. Switch 2 was a massive disappointment. No innovation or anything just slightly better specs. Nintendo has ran out of ideas and are in the milking phase of their company, and the cows utters are almost dry

62264949
>>62263652
buy the dip is for spy, anon

62265067
>>62264904
Reversing to sell PS6, then unreversing. Nah, not gonna fall for it.

62266693
>>62259371
>the company's tremendous long-term upside
so you think they're goin to start announcing good games any minute now huh

62266717
>>62266693
It's not about the games, retard. Yeah the games sucks now - doesn't matter. The reason to buy Nintendo now is because Pokémon cards are booming. The "but the games suck now" argument against Nintendo now while completely ignoring the record shattering success of Pokémon cards reminds me of the "but Facebook sucks now" argument against Meta a few years ago while completely ignoring the record shattering success of Instagram.

62266743
>>62259371
Completely agree. Mario movie universe will make billions every movie

62268236
>>62261625
why did their stock crash when their consoles and games sell a lot?

item.title: /Biz revival — 90 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-05-24 00:47 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266173
>>62265918
it went from like 17 cents to 1.50 or something still proftable but you arent wrong its a dead shitcoin as I said wait till the economy shits itself first
BTC dumped to 15k when Oil spiked under biden if you recall I expect another big turnup for oil like precious metals did

62266175
>>62265634
OP *is* the spammer

62266178
>>62265918
Id make fun of you for holding my nuts let be honest here.

62266203
>>62265467
hey man, I hope you get cancer

62266239
We’ve all moved to Twitter and telegram you fucking retard. Imagine still holding tech alts like link and faggot ass lte. If you’re in crypto and you don’t either 1) buy memecoins or 2) haven’t picked up perp trading bitcoin, you’re fucking hopeless. This place will never be what it used to be because of you FAGGOT JEET JANNIES KILL YOURSELVES. That’s the most you’ll get out of me. I’ll go back to never posting again because anyone left posting here is a braindead monkey.
>hurrdurrr my hype is up 50 percent
That’s cute. My troll and neet is up like a thousand percent.

62266242
>>62266203
Hah, no no it was me he was calling an aids infected faggot, I'm the anon who nearly died
>btw OP was at my doctors getting augmentin since I'm blowing huge clots out of my nose.
I don't mind him, least he's forcing engagement which is better than most people here.
At this point it's friendly banter.

62266392
In reality we need a revival of the human spirit, money is mostly pointless and only a currency used to exchange for goods and services, but in reality it's even less than that it's more akin to a warm fire or a security blanket.
What men are lacking is love for each other, not in the reddit gay sense either but as others said putting value in people.
Even OP who I was in sincere disagreement with has value, he pushed me to change my current circumstances by shifting my behavior.
I wasn't doing well the other day for a multitude of deep reasons, if OP even knew he wouldn't probably have had such harsh discourse with me but I don't even mind because every action has a reaction.

And I thought it was cute when I saw him actually worried if I had done something, he's on the road despite his anger, this place gambling/money changing in general tends to bring the worst out in us.
https://youtu.be/0x1StsL1zok

A revival of brotherhood is more important than the revival on making money, but even OP is craving that in some deeper intention, community is what you seek, but you do need to seek from within first to realize there's no value in this world if we can't see the potential in other people.

For example in the medical field I don't get much sincere engagement with doctors, I'm a complicated patient which scares doctors away from usually treating me with digity, and even worse usually treating me at all due to fear of being sued.
But one of my doctors who treats me like a human I saw today and gave me what I needed, because he knows the human side of medicine, he puts value in me because I bring him a unique side of medicine most doctors don't get to see (extremely rare syndromes) while also hearing about my personal life which he's amazed I'm able to do much at all considering how much I have on my plate, he met me after two major car accidents I was involved in before knowing I was a case study.

62266433
>>62265467
I respect you for trying to do something positive. /biz/ does suck ass lately.

In anycase, I'll ask a question. Why the hell is NEAR pumping that hard? Are people actually using it?

62266436
>>62265525
do NOT buy RBLX for more than a trade. accounting fraud

62266459
>>62266392
ok baldie, can you try to bring actual financial value instead of constantly whining like a bitch about your miserable existence?
We're not doing communism. Socialized healthcare is ok in fact i already have it so i don't think about it at all.

62266474
>>62266459
Where are you from, canada?
and no not /pol/ the fact you still think in isms is something.

62266478
>>62266459
Why do you have that saved.....

62266483
>>62266478
>62266478
zesty buckos are fun. And i can always post this when a brown person attacks the white race. I like your pepe, adding it to my meme folder

62266484
>>62266459
>>62266474
>>62266478
Third question are you the anime posting zoomer anon asking for tiktok links the other day?
Since there's only three of us here now.

62266493
>>62266483
If you're zesty buck posting should at the very least have the two where the twink buck is feeding the faggot who somehow looks like a lesbian woman.

62266502
>>62266484
no

from western europe btw. Not telling you were it's a faggy country
>>62266493
post it. do u post on /pol too? ibet you goon to hentai, never visited any other board than pol or biz

62266521
>>62266502
No I actually was the anon who got people to turn on tranime posting here lol, I never got into anime besides Berserk but that feels like gospel at this point everyone knows that.
I don't have it saved lol, I came from /pol/ after being a lolbert activist in the 2010s and stumbled my way almost to 100k in 4 months of being on /biz/ from being broke.
Then lost most of it, then met multiple millionaires, and then talked to Mark Cuban during the bullrun it was a wild experience.

I don't really post on /pol/ unless Israel is being bombed.

62266580
>>62266521
yes pol is too cringe and brown nowadays, i still go there to hurl racial insults from time to time and uplift my mood.
Biz is also very bad with 0 financial value but occasionnaly you make a valuable discovery. it's where i discovered LITS (yeah i know you don't like litecoin)
The reality is that it takes a lot of digging to find gems.

62266591
>>62266580
I'm just giving you shit, I've made money off litecoin multiple times.
and still the cheapest to transfer money around like for example you need to move 10k from coinbase to kraken, using BTC or ETH is gonna cost you an arm and a leg.
LTC is remarkably cheaper.

62266600
>>62266580
Last attempt I had was 10k on a coin that just released all Althea on Kraken, absolutely got buttfucked bought at 4 dollars when it released 6 dollars, looked like a solid project and was early............
...
Dropped to 4 cents think it's still there, but probably a good investment now, not when I bought.

62266609
An anon tries to create a decent thread and it gets instantly sabotaged.

62266618
>>62266600
>>62266591
that's why i buy litecoin. I don't want to buy something that will go to 0, i'm not looking for a x100 which is too hard to find.
Rn at 50$ ltc, has very little downside and a lot of upside. Even if it does a 3x at best, that's a relatively safe and ez 3x. Of course it could go lower even 0 everything is possible but highly unlikely.
And it could moon in the future because everyone uses it and somehow it hasn't shined yet

62266643
>>62265467
Akash, DePin actually seems to be getting some traction

62267090
>>62266618
it would have to get hacked or somehow so toxic everyone delists it even then only way its going to 0 is if btc goes to 0

62267267
>>62267090
i'll look into it but it's already 2x / 3x from bottom

62267325
>>62267267
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4vF3o4Ss6g

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62266460

62266464

item.title: There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a CSR/AMEX Plat/CapOne VX — 17 replies — 4.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a CSR/AMEX Plat/CapOne VX

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-24 09:12 UTC

OP

There are people on this board who are so poor they don't have a CSR/AMEX Plat/CapOne VX

Early replies (first 3)

62267309
>no Revolut metal Visa Infinite

62267314
>>62267305
SAAAR

62267315
>>62267305
what phenotype has the rounded thumbnail?
I'm scots-irish and my thumbnail is almost flat across the front

also everyone here hates debt to the point of it being some sort of compulsion. Even if they are rich, they don't have credit cards.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267316
I have about 120k in credit between all of these. If I max them out and then move to montenegro can they ever get me?

62267317
>>62267305
>paying to use a credit card

KWAB
My bank pays me

62267334
>>62267305
anyone can get those cards.

62267350
>>62267315
>what phenotype has the rounded thumbnail?
nevermind I googled the name on the card

GR8 B8 M8 I R8 8 out of 8

62267470
>>62267305
I only accept fees to use peoples cards, I don't pay them.

62267608
>>62267305
way to signal all the browns that you're the perfect target

62267625
>>62267305
>Muh credit
I don't borrow, I just use money.

62267629
>poor
That's not how credit cards work retard. How high your income is has nothing to do with getting these cards. I unironically have below minimum wage income and I have them.

62267873
>>62267305
that's an anagram for penes, k

62267903
>>62267629
>>62267305
Yeah Amex will hand the platinum card to anyone with a pulse able to cough up the annual fee. There's only a handful of cards that you actually need a high net worth to have and a lot of the benefits are pretty mediocre.

62268146
>>62267305
I have a friend who has the schwab amex plat, but because he has more than 10m in Schwab they pay him to use it or something.

62268153
>>62267316
lmao I wouldn't move to Montenegro for 240k

62268155
>>62267305
I wonder how much 4chan gets paid for posts like this. AFAIK ads for these cards pay really well.

>>62267315
>what phenotype has the rounded thumbnail?
indians kek

62268498
I have
SoFi unlimited credit card
SoFi debit checking card
Current credit builder card with numbers painted over as my main food card so melinated individuals cant snap photos

item.title: USD/TRY — 6 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
USD/TRY

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-24 11:25 UTC

OP

Okay, I need your honestest onions. Would you borrow liras and buy dollars looking at this chart?
They sold nearly all US Treasuries.

Early replies (first 3)

62265161
>>62265133
prob best to short lira

62265993
>>62265161
That's literally what he said nigger

62266216
>>62265133
Something about a steamroller and pennies and negative skew should come to mind.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266255
obviously, the moment you try shorting you will be shafted instantly

62268084
>>62265993
lol

62268661
>>62265133
borrowing lira costs alot tho

item.title: Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th… — 60 replies — 4.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th…

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-05-23 21:49 UTC

OP

Anon, its my bday today
Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in the bank, had a successful career but then pivoted to make a startup and lost shit ton of money and time in the process. Not sure what to do anymore. Basically going through the dark night of the soul.

Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US to not die on the street. You are, as usual, my only real friend.

Tell me something nice or not.

Early replies (first 3)

62261420
>>62261414
>Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US
Are you a foreigner? Is this a LARP?

62261422
You did good

62261428
>>62261414
Your life can turn around in a week easy. New job, new girl, market pumps, whatever. Don't be so down anon you don't know what is around the corner. Also you're only early 30s, you're young as fuck, you have to live as long as you already have all over again before you hit mid 60s, just think how long that truly is. Stop worrying and enjoy your life you fag.

Happy Birthday, enjoy the magic trick.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263944
>>62262866
Glad you liked it.
I was banging an 18 year old last year but got tired of it.
She had a kid already right after

62263952
>>62263942
but now you owe double taxes typically, or if you go back home, and cost of living in the US is more than likely higher than euros
I should have been a software fag, instead im a sickfag
this place is insanely expensive, how you enjoying the US?
Btw they just converted most gas to e-15 overnight watch it with your car, it can slag certain engines, this place is going to shit fast.

62263962
>>62263952
>Owe double taxes
No I don’t. Ireland and the USA have tax treaties. The cost of living where I live (east coast) is high but does not scale to eat up all of my income, otherwise I wouldn’t have moved here.
I am thoroughly enjoying the USA and wish I could stay but unfortunately my company are a bunch of cunts and don’t sponsor people. Been grand so far. Despite all of the stereotypes I work with geniuses in my field and there’s not that many turbo fatties.

62263984
what was the startup idea?

62264000
>>62263962
I figured you would like it, i have a friend Im trying to convince to move here, I'd sponsor him though I'm sure his work would.

If you get a chance to come back shoot for the midwest, Cerner has a lot of software jobs though might have changed names, cost of living is quite a bit less and still a chance to get a good home for decent pricing.

62264015
>>62264000
I’m not a software engineer, I’m a chemical engineer. Also, I’m supposed to move to the Midwest for the last year of my visa. Missouri.

62264068
>>62264015
There ya go I live in KC, there's a bayer plant I believe not too far off from me.

62264074
>>62264015
Also spray me with your poisons mister.

62264117
>>62261414
so you had one. stop whining you made your choices

62264167
>>62261501
worst cat picture

62264433
>>62263944
>banging an 18 year old last year
Schizo head canon until proven otherwise

62264610
>>62261414
You are a baby at 30

62264632
>>62261414
K-K-K-K-K-K-KWAB

62264651
>>62264074
>>62264068
No way. We should become bros when I move there.

62264653
Buy a pressure washer

62264665
>>62261414
You sound like a normal person.
The average person with a “wife” or “gf” are in a desperate position to keep their partner and has no leverage over the relationship anyways.
The average salary is $65k and is heavily pulled up by high earners.

62264676
>>62261414
even through the darkest nights, self improve. Focus on becoming better and growing more knowledge and experience. It will be the only thing you can take with you after death. Then you get to become a part of me. You all will as I will you

62264696
>>62264651
lol might be related I'm irish on both sides of my fathers side then again on my mothers, O'Noonans/Picts.
If you get your citizenship housing is still very cheap here and plenty of small cozy towns with 400-900 people only an hour or so from the big city.

62264705
>>62264433
lol she was a depressed black girl I met doordashing, was stressful due to one her being so young, and two her mom was my age lol.

62264708
>>62264433
I'd have to drop her off around the corner, wasn't illegal obviously but if her dad and mom had found out I was their age I'm fairly sure the dad might have shot me.
Luckily nobody ever caught wind of it, but she kept doing LSD with her friends and then call me asking to drive her away because shes having bad trips, I wasn't into it, I pushed her to date a guy she liked growing up with because it was the right thing to do, I didn't however expect her to get pregnant immediately, oh well she has some pretty bad beef curtains, but sadly think it was natural.

62264726
Sorry bros if I don't respond I got a terrible migraine today it happens a lot to me, but I got some dino nuggies and gonna sleep after.
It's interesting now when i get migrianes which might be stemming from the tumor in my fucking neck I feel this invisible crown on my head.
Almost like wearing a baseball cap except it's invisible the sensation of wearing a crown from my skull.
Then usually mind bending pain and nausea, once it happened to me in 2023 and I think my kidney was infected but I saw Ophainm and a green eye eminating from the middle of it looking at me.
Was the only hallucination I've ever experienced, and some out of body experiences during a bone marrow biopsy where I could see them drilling into my spine.
If I survive much longer I'm going to be shocked this surgery should have killed my ass.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3hnCT4yx0TM

62264747
And sorry for hijacking your thread if it is your birthday anon.
Have a pepe, your birthday is the same as my xister, who I can't really stand.

62264759
>>62261414
If it is your goal to improve, you will, even slowly. But it will not fall into your lap, at home especially. You will look back at these times and laugh.

62264763
>>62264696
What is Lenexa/ Shawnee/ Olathe like?

62264802
>>62264763
Rich and jewish, so downtown KC was all jews for a long time and grandview, after everything started getting older and more blacks and mexicans moved in they all uprooted and went to "leawood" but now leawoods getting old, expensive houses but now likely getting old, similar with Lenexa, Overland park is nice but again this is like a super rich migrated area so everythings much more expensive that being said there is a wonderful sushi restaurant in Lenexa named Sushi Uni, if you were to pick one I'd go Lenexa, it's less expensive than the leawood area.
Kansas taxes are shit, missouri has legal weed and lower gas prices and tobacco/housing.

The smarter people live in Lee's summit where I grew up but it's getting little crowded.
I'm actually thinking about leaving the city myself I had a farm as a kid in El dorado springs but my dad sold it to piss my mom off.

62264879
>>62261512
Deporting the illegals, lower taxes/higher pay, owning guns, etc…

62265650
>>62262803
>30y old.
>You are baby.
What's up with unc copes lately? 30 is when your life is basically over and your duty is to nourish the next generation.

62265678
>>62261414
>had a great career but thought I was smarter than I actually am so I tried to start my own business and failed
>could’ve had a cozy life but got greedy
>let me go to my favorite racist pedo chat board to complain

62265707
>>62261414
Man I wish I was 30 again, I could fix everything in my life. Happy Birthday

62267276
>>62265678
This. Instagram grifters mind broke many men into
>having a business is how you make it
which is not exactly false, but not truthful either
